messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] FOSDEM 2027 - 1st Bioinformatics and Computational Biology Developer Room
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] FOSDEM 2027 - 1st Bioinformatics and Computational Biology Developer Room
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone, the largest free and open source  meeting in Europe in 2026
will have a Bioinformatics/Computational Biology devroom!

The CfP for talks is now open:
https://fosdem-bioinformatics.github.io/cfp.html and it would be nice to
have some talks about new (or not!) Bioconductor packages.

I look forward to seeing some fellow bioconductor developers in Bruxelles,
E.
-- 
RTDA at University of Torino, Department of Oncology
Candiolo Cancer Institute, FPO-IRCCS
Laboratory of Translational Cancer Medicine
$ pom

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
Hi everyone, the largest free and open source  meeting in Europe in 2026
will have a Bioinformatics/Computational Biology devroom!

The CfP for talks is now open:
https://fosdem-bioinformatics.github.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Request to Sync GitHub Repository with Bioconductor for Package "Aerith"
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Request to Sync GitHub Repository with Bioconductor for Package "Aerith"
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Core Team,

I am the maintainer of the Bioconductor package Aerith, currently hosted at:
? https://github.com/xyz1396/Aerith

The package was submitted through the Bioconductor Contributions tracker:
? https://github.com/Bioconductor/Contributions/issues/3828

I would like to request assistance in syncing the GitHub repository with the Bioconductor Git repository to ensure consistency between the two versions.

I am unable to resolve the following error from the build server on my end:

Files over the limit:
 /home/pkgbuild/packagebuilder/workers/jobs/3828/fb5b4dcb8b4e61cda0d7be55261151f72d5a2504/Aerith/.git/objects/pack/pack-f582d80500050fb2ef9282997e11aec8a9aaac61.pack
 /home/pkgbuild/packagebuilder/workers/jobs/3828/fb5b4dcb8b4e61cda0d7be55261151f72d5a2504/Aerith/.git/objects/pack/pack-f582d80500050fb2ef9282997e11aec8a9aaac61.pack
 /home/pkgbuild/packagebuilder/workers/jobs/3828/fb5b4dcb8b4e61cda0d7be55261151f72d5a2504/Aerith/.git/objects/pack/pack-f582d80500050fb2ef9282997e11aec8a9aaac61.pack

Please let me know if any additional steps or information are required on my end.

Thank you for your time and support.

Best regards,
Yi Xiong


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Problems fixing error package 'dominatR'
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Core Team,

I am the maintainer of the newly accepted package dominatR, currently
present at

https://github.com/VanBortleLab/dominatR

The package was submitted and accepted in Bioconductor. This is the Issue
for tracking:

https://github.com/Bioconductor/Contributions/issues/3938

I was checking the building report on Bioconductor and there is one error
during the building of the package

https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.23/bioc-LATEST/dominatR/nebbiolo1-checksrc.html

The problem, I thik it is very simple, but I am new on all this and I am a
little bit confused about how to fix it, given that I didn't have this
problem during the review process!.

I would appreciate your help on this matter

Thank you so much!

* checking re-building of vignette outputs ... ERROR
Error(s) in re-building vignettes:
  ...
--- re-building ?dominatR.Rmd? using rmarkdown
File ../man/figures/dominatR_logo.png not found in resource path
Error: processing vignette 'dominatR.Rmd' failed with diagnostics:
pandoc document conversion failed with error 99
--- failed re-building ?dominatR.Rmd?

SUMMARY: processing the following file failed:
  ?dominatR.Rmd?

Error: Vignette re-building failed.
Execution halted

* checking PDF version of manual ... OK
* DONE

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
Dear Bioconductor Core Team,

I am the maintainer of the newly accepted package dominatR, currently
present at

https://github.com/VanBortleLab/dominatR

The package was submitted and accepted in Bioc

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Problems fixing error package 'dominatR'
messages.body_plain:
Not sure why this didn't occur during the review process but on quick glance it seems like you have a hard coded path to a figure in your man directory of your package.  This would not be transportable/finable on other systems. You should include the image in say inst/images or inst/extdata and then use a system.file call to retrieve the file on any system.

Cheers,


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Maintainers on CBN2Path
messages.body_plain:
Good afternoon,

I am trying to add a second maintainer to CBN2Path, but those changes
appear to be causing a DESCRIPTION file error. Is it possible to have
multiple maintainers and if so, how should that be done? At the moment I'm
just applying the "cre" role to both authors.

Best regards,
William C

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
Good afternoon,

I am trying to add a second maintainer to CBN2Path, but those changes
appear to be causing a DESCRIPTION file error. Is it possible to have
multiple maintainers and if so, how should

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Maintainers on CBN2Path
messages.body_plain:
Authors at R  only allows one maintainer.  We encourage good coding practice to manage changes from multiple contributors on a repo through pull requests (PR) managed by the maintainer and the maintainer push changes to the Bioconductor location.

If multiple push access directly to Bioconductor is required, we can assist with this.  A request by the current maintainter to add access is reuqired. We would need to create BiocCredential account for additional maintainers; we would either need the email associated with and existing BiocCredentials account or the email/github id information for new users to create one.

Cheers,


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

________________________________
From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of William Choi-Kim <will.choi.kim at gmail.com>
Sent: Thursday, November 6, 2025 5:28 PM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] Maintainers on CBN2Path

Good afternoon,

I am trying to add a second maintainer to CBN2Path, but those changes
appear to be causing a DESCRIPTION file error. Is it possible to have
multiple maintainers and if so, how should that be done? At the moment I'm
just applying the "cre" role to both authors.

Best regards,
William C

        [[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Announcing the EuroBioC2026 Sticker Design Contest!
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Announcing the EuroBioC2026 Sticker Design Contest!
messages.body_plain:
We invite creative minds to design the official sticker for EuroBioC2026, scheduled to take place in June 2026 in Turku, Finland.

How to Enter:
Submit your sticker design using this form by January 31st, 2026: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe6RZlyUspUBZAYKzjX_ctHg3HO-3I1D2y5YTGOTwj8-z6R_Q/viewform

Prizes:
? Free registration to EuroBioC2026 for the winning design.
? The winning sticker will be featured on the conference website and printed for attendees at the event.

We can?t wait to see your designs!


Laurah Ondari
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)





	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
We invite creative minds to design the official sticker for EuroBioC2026, scheduled to take place in June 2026 in Turku, Finland.

How to Enter:
Submit your sticker design using this form by January 3

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Seminar Series - Dec 2: Deep-learning Gene Perturbation Effect Prediction
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Seminar Series - Dec 2: Deep-learning Gene Perturbation Effect Prediction
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor developers,

Join us for the first session in the new Bioconductor Seminar Series, designed to connect cutting-edge research with Bioconductor development.

Topic:
Deep-learning-based Gene Perturbation Effect Prediction Does Not Yet Outperform Simple Linear Baselines (Nature Methods, 2025)

Date & Time:
December 2, 2025
9 PM CET / 3 PM EST / 12 PM PST

Speakers:

  *
Constantin Ahlmann-Eltze (University College London)
  *
Wolfgang Huber (EMBL)
  *
Simon Anders (University of Heidelberg)

Discussant:
Davide Risso (University of Padova)

Format:
30 min insights ? 15 min guided discussion ? 15 min open Q&A

Register: https://dfci.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_0nAabJt1Qeyd8QsJjvEzOQ
Paper: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12328236/


Maria Doyle, PhD

Bioconductor Community Manager



School of Medicine,

University of Limerick, Limerick, V94 T9PX
Ireland

Email: maria.doyle at ul.ie<mailto:maria.doyle at ul.ie>
Phone (office): +353 61 234 768

Connect with the Bioconductor community at our conferences:

  *   BioCAsia 2025 (November 27-28, Adelaide, Australia)<https://biocasia2025.bioconductor.org/>
  *
EuroBioC2026 (June 3-5, Turku, Finland)<https://eurobioc2026.bioconductor.org/>
  *
BioC2026 (August 10-12, Seattle, USA)<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/>



I work flexible hours across several time zones. I don't expect you to read or respond to my emails outside of your normal working hours.
messages.body_snippet:
Dear Bioconductor developers,

Join us for the first session in the new Bioconductor Seminar Series, designed to connect cutting-edge research with Bioconductor development.

Topic:
Deep-learning-base

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Issue Activating Bioconductor Git Credentials Account
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Issue Activating Bioconductor Git Credentials Account
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Team,

I am writing to request assistance with activating my Bioconductor Git
Credentials account. When I attempted to activate my account using the
email *hudie.luoluo at gmail.com*, I received the following message:

"hudie.luoluo at gmail.com is not associated with a maintainer of a
Bioconductor package."

I am the maintainer of the package *EMTscoreData,* and this is the email
address listed in the package?s DESCRIPTION file as well as my GitHub
account. Could you please help verify the email associated with my
maintainer account or update it if necessary?

Thank you very much for your help.

Best regards,
haimei

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel]  Issue Activating Bioconductor Git Credentials Account
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel]  Issue Activating Bioconductor Git Credentials Account
messages.body_plain:
Thank you for your inquiry.
So recapping:  You were able to join the bioc-devel at r-project.org mailing list?

The BiocCredentials account has not yet been created as it looks like your package is still in the awaiting moderation phase of review.  The package has not yet been added to our git.bioconductor.org repository.  Once this happens a BiocCredentials account will automatically be created and you would then be able to activate it.  For now in the submission process, please fix any pre-review comments on your github repository.

Cheers,



Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Single Package Builder - Updates and Interruption
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Single Package Builder - Updates and Interruption
messages.body_plain:
Over the next few days we will be updating the Single Package Builder for incoming Bioconductor submissions to be running on R-devel and Bioc 3.23 devel.    During this time period there may be periods that the on demand builds for incoming submissions may not be available or working properly.   Thank you for your understanding and patiences during this time period.


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] EuroBioC2026 – Call for Abstracts
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor developers,

The EuroBioC2026 Call for Abstracts is Now Open!

We invite submissions for short talks, posters, and workshops for EuroBioC2026.

 ?? Important Dates
? 24 November 2025: Call opens
? 30 January 2026: Call closes

? Submission Types
? Short talks: 10-minute presentations followed by Q&A, focused on packages, research questions, or technology that motivates software development.
? Posters: Classical A0 portrait posters presented during the poster session.
? Workshops: 1.5-hour hands-on, interactive sessions demonstrating a software package or workflow.

Submit your abstract here: https://openreview.net/group?id=bioconductor.org/EuroBioC/2026/Conference

?? Note: The OpenReview submission system requires all submitters to create an account at least two weeks before the abstract deadline. Please register early to avoid issues.

For any questions, contact eurobioc at bioconductor.org

Best regards,
Laurah Ondari
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)
